events.title: Memory Cradle
events.description: Ongoing through July 18 at Eleanor Harwood Gallery within Minnesota Street Project. Works exploring memory, material, and personal archive. Wed–Sat 11am–5pm.

events.title: Mark Perlman — Recent Works
events.description: Ongoing through July 18 at Nancy Toomey Fine Art, Minnesota Street Project. Paintings drawing from landscape and abstraction.

events.title: Zach Noe Towers
events.description: Friday run continues. Two shows: 7:30 PM and 9:45 PM.

events.title: This Charming Band
events.description: One of the most accurate Smiths/Morrissey tribute acts in the world, consistently sold-out at GAMH. 'There Is a Light That Never Goes Out' performed exactly as intended.

events.title: SF Pride Concert – San Francisco Opera
events.description: Conductor Robert Mollicone leads the SF Opera Orchestra with soloists Melody Moore, Nikola Printz, and Reginald Smith, Jr. blending opera, musical theater, and pop. Drag queen emcee Sapphira Cristál, post-show dance party with DJ Juanita More.

events.title: Bird & Beckett – Jazz in the Bookshop (Friday series)
events.description: Bird & Beckett's regular Friday evening jazz series in their cozy bookshop. Intimate live jazz in a neighborhood setting. Reservations recommended.

events.title: ICA SF – Tara Donovan, Jeffrey Gibson, Lily Kwong
events.venue: ICA SF (multiple sites incl. Transamerica Pyramid & Port of SF)
events.description: ICA SF's citywide exhibitions: Tara Donovan's 'Stratagems,' Jeffrey Gibson's 'THIS BURNING WORLD (2026),' Lily Kwong's EARTHSEED DOME, and Patrick Martinez's 'Dogpatch Community Court.' No admission charge.

events.title: Minnesota Street Project – Adler Guerrier & Group Shows
events.description: Adler Guerrier: 'Prim and Mottled Forms' at re.riddle; 'How to See Ruth Asawa' in the Media Gallery (through Jul 20); Memory Cradle and Mark Perlman at Eleanor Harwood and Nancy Toomey Fine Art.

events.title: SF Pride Heart of the City – Art Show & Makers Market
events.description: Pride-themed art opening and makers market blending visual art, handmade goods, and community. Runs 5–9 PM.

events.title: Trans March & Festival
events.description: One of the world's largest trans-specific gatherings. Stage, performers, and resources fair 3–6 PM at Dolores Park, then march to Civic Center at 6 PM.

events.title: Dance Fridays
events.description: SF's long-running weekly salsa and Latin dance event. Lessons at 7:45 PM, social dancing until late. All levels, large floor, excellent DJs.

events.title: Drag Me Downtown – SF Pride Pop-Up Drag Show
events.description: Pride drag performance series with 4 shows and 13 performers. 100% SF performers in a pop-up outdoor setting. Runs 5–7 PM.

events.title: Free Bookshop Comedy – Pride Edition
events.description: Free stand-up comedy show at Adobe Books. This Pride Edition raises funds for LYRIC SF to support unhoused queer and trans youth. All ages.

events.title: Golden Gate Comedy Night
events.description: Intimate free comedy showcase featuring top Bay Area comedians. Two-drink minimum. Runs through 10:30 PM.

events.title: Your Fucked Up Relationship – Improv Comedy
events.description: Long-running improv comedy show (since 2011) where audience relationship stories inspire improvised scenes. 9–10:15 PM.

events.title: Zach Noe Towers
events.description: Saturday closing night of Zach Noe Towers' Punch Line run.

events.title: Wheelchair Sports Camp
events.description: Oakland hip-hop collective led by Kalyn Heffernan — wheelchair user, disability justice advocate, and genuinely powerful performer. Critically acclaimed live show.

events.title: Jazz in the Bookshop – B3B4
events.description: B3B4 plays Hammond organ jazz — Kevin Gerzevitz on organ, Tom Griesser on saxophone, Scott Foster on guitar, Dan Foltz on drums. Intimate jazz in a beloved bookshop.

events.title: Pier Party at the Wharf
events.description: Free outdoor summer concert and dance party featuring DJ Umami, live band Analog Dog, and Drag on Deck. Runs 2–6 PM every 2nd and 4th Saturday.

events.title: SF Pride Festival at Civic Center
events.description: Two-day Pride festival. Main Stage noon–6 PM plus 5 community stages with local LGBTQ+ performers and DJs. One of the last major Pride celebrations that remains completely free.

events.title: Moleskine Project XIII
events.description: 13th edition of the global Moleskine artist collaboration — curated works in Moleskine notebooks by artists worldwide. Opened June 27, on view through July.

events.title: de Young Free Saturday
events.description: Free general admission to permanent galleries every Saturday for 9-county Bay Area residents. Show Bay Area ID. Special exhibitions still require a ticket.

events.title: Introductions – Ruth Asawa School of the Arts
events.description: Opening reception for student and faculty works from Ruth Asawa School of the Arts — visual arts, architecture & design, costume & fashion. Remarks at 4:30 PM.

events.title: STUDIO Gallery – Coming Attractions Opening
events.description: Opening reception 3–5 PM for 'Coming Attractions' — group exhibition featuring Julia Foug, Robert Frank, and Mila Kirillova. Runs through July 13.

events.title: Creative Youth Celebration – YBCA
events.venue: Yerba Buena Center for the Arts
events.description: One-day showcase of award-winning high school artists across film, music, writing, photography, dance, and visual art.

events.title: Yes, Clef – Improvised Musical Comedy
events.description: Fully improvised musical comedy show. Followed by 'Brief Tiny World' (8 PM) and 'Super Scene' (9 PM) on the same ticket at The Eagle Theater.

events.title: Japanese Breakfast at Stern Grove
events.description: Michelle Zauner's indie rock project headlining Stern Grove's free outdoor amphitheater. Lottery tickets sold out — check wait list or arrive early for standby.

events.title: Golden Gate Park Band – Around the World in 90 Minutes
events.description: Free outdoor concert featuring Haydn's Trumpet Concerto, Gottschalk, Saint-Saëns, Strauss, Beatles, and Prokofiev's Peter and the Wolf narrated by KDFC's Maggie Clennon Reberg. Sundays 1–2:30 PM.

events.title: SF Pride Festival at Civic Center – Day 2
events.description: Pride Parade along Market Street at 10:30 AM followed by festival at Civic Center until 6 PM. Five stages with headliners and community performers.

events.title: Scott Amendola / Kasey Knudsen / Mat Muntz – Farewell Set
events.description: Special farewell performance from the trio of Scott Amendola (drums), Kasey Knudsen (reeds), and Mat Muntz — part of a week-long run of events at Bird & Beckett. Experimental jazz and improvisation.

events.title: Bird & Beckett Sunday Jazz
events.description: Weekly Sunday afternoon jazz sessions at the beloved Glen Park bookstore and record shop. Intimate space, rotating local musicians.

events.title: El Rio – ENVY Sunday Dance Party
events.description: ENVY is El Rio's Pride weekend Sunday afternoon dance party. Beloved Mission queer dive with outdoor patio.

events.title: SF Comedy Showcase
events.description: Rotating lineup of top Bay Area stand-up comedians at Punch Line. A great entry point to the SF comedy scene.

events.title: SalsaCrazy Mondays
events.description: Weekly salsa social in the Richmond. Class at 6:30 PM, social dancing until 10 PM. Multi-level, welcoming to newcomers. 20+ years running.

events.title: Hayes Valley Comedy Night
events.description: Free Monday night comedy showcase featuring rotating local stand-up comedians. Casual neighborhood bar vibe.

events.title: Mirthquake: Monday Live Comedy
events.description: Free weekly Monday comedy show at the Abbey Tavern in the Richmond. Intimate bar setting with up-and-coming Bay Area comedians.

events.title: Comedy Allstars
events.description: Rotating ensemble of top Bay Area and touring stand-up comedians. Punch Line's Monday showcase format.

events.title: SF Comedy Kings: An Old School Blowout
events.description: Themed comedy event celebrating the classic era of stand-up with established SF Bay Area comedians delivering old-school style sets.

events.title: Chaunte Wayans – Stand-Up Comedy
events.description: Member of the legendary Wayans comedy family performs sharp observational comedy on family, life, and pop culture at Cobb's in North Beach.

events.title: Patrick Keane – Stand-Up Comedy
events.description: Comedian Patrick Keane takes the stage at Punch Line. Also performing Friday July 3.

events.title: Fillmore Jazz Festival
events.description: West Coast's largest free jazz festival — 5 stages across 12 blocks with 30+ acts including Adam Theis's MilesTrain Project (Miles Davis tribute), Lavay Smith & Her Red Hot Skillet Lickers, and more. Runs July 4–5.

events.title: Bird & Beckett – Jazz Club Saturday Night
events.description: Bird & Beckett's Saturday Jazz Club series. Rotating local jazz artists in an intimate bookshop setting. A Glen Park institution since 2002.

events.title: de Young Free Saturday
events.description: Free general admission every Saturday for residents of the nine Bay Area counties. Show Bay Area ID at the door.

events.title: Major Lazer at Stern Grove
events.description: Global dance trio of Diplo, Walshy Fire, and Ape Drums headlining Stern Grove. Support: Fijiana (Indo-Fijian rapper from Richmond CA) and DJ Bad Juuju. Doors noon, show 2 PM.

events.title: Fillmore Jazz Festival – Day 2
events.description: Second day of the Fillmore Jazz Festival. Five stages across 12 blocks. Art, crafts, food vendors alongside 30+ performers.

events.title: After the End of the World Coretet Reunion
events.description: Reunion performance: Jon Birdsong (brass), Annelise Zamula (reeds), Bill Noertker (strings), Dave Mihaly (percussion). Experimental jazz in an intimate bookshop.

events.title: SF Comedy Showcase
events.description: Sunday night lineup of Bay Area's best stand-up comedians at Punch Line.
